ground_truth.title: Best Travel Insurance for Seniors in 2025
ground_truth.author: Jeff Hoyt
ground_truth.main_content: If you plan on traveling abroad or domestically, we recommend purchasing travel insurance for some added peace of mind. Travel insurance offers protection when you find yourself in an unexpected situation, such as an injury that requires emergency medical care, lost luggage, or canceled flights. Our team spent over 100 hours researching and comparing plans before choosing Allianz as our top pick for their optional preexisting condition coverage and excellent travel expense protection. We evaluated each plan using important factors like cost, customer service, plan options, coverage amounts, and more. We chose Allianz, a trusted travel insurance provider for over a century, because many of their policies offer protection for those with preexisting medical conditions. Just remember that this coverage is only available with select plans, and there are some exclusions. Allianz offers 24/7 assistance and a variety of plans, whether you’re planning a single trip or multiple trips with several destinations. One of their most popular plans is OneTrip Prime, which provides free coverage for children 17 and under when traveling with a parent or grandparent. Keep in mind that select benefits may not be available to residents of some states. Established in 1990 in Indianapolis, Indiana, International Medical Group (IMG) is known for offering quality global insurance benefits and assistance for seniors who travel internationally. The company offers a wide range of international medical insurance and 24/7 emergency medical assistance. There are also many options for people with unique needs and travel plans — from one-time travelers to long-term expats. IMG earned our pick as the best international travel insurance for their affordable rates for longer trips, along with comprehensive coverage for medical expenses and evacuation. We appreciate that IMG offers GlobeHopper Senior, temporary health insurance for travelers ages 65 and up traveling internationally. IMG also offers travel medical insurance for individuals, families, and groups, including — like Allianz — coverage for preexisting conditions. Many people trust John Hancock for their investment, retirement, financial, and insurance products. An insurance provider for over 160 years, John Hancock offers streamlined travel insurance packages with ample protection for older adults. Although on the pricier side, John Hancock has three straightforward plans ranked Gold, Silver, and Bronze, depending on how much coverage you need. John Hancock’s plans feature comprehensive coverage, including trip cancellation coverage, trip delay coverage, emergency accident and sickness medical coverage, and more. They also offer high coverage options and greater protection for seniors who want 24/7 assistance. Nationwide is a well-known insurance provider that offers a variety of travel insurance plans for seniors. We chose Nationwide as the best cruise travel insurance for seniors because they offer specialized cruise plans with coverage for missed port departures, itinerary changes, and cruise ship disablement. Nationwide also offers a variety of other travel insurance plans, including single-trip, multi-trip, and annual plans. Travel Guard is a travel insurance provider that offers a variety of plans for seniors, including single-trip, multi-trip, and annual plans. We chose Travel Guard as the best for specialized coverages because they offer a wide range of optional coverages, such as rental car damage, accidental death and dismemberment, and trip cancellation for any reason. Travel Guard also offers 24/7 assistance and a variety of plans to fit different budgets and needs. Travel insurance typically covers trip cancellation, trip interruption, trip delay, missed connection, baggage loss, baggage delay, emergency medical expenses, emergency medical evacuation, and accidental death and dismemberment. Some plans also offer coverage for preexisting medical conditions, rental car damage, and trip cancellation for any reason. It’s important to read the fine print of your policy to understand what is and isn’t covered. The cost of travel insurance for seniors varies depending on a number of factors, including the traveler’s age, destination, length of trip, and the type of coverage selected. In general, travel insurance for seniors is more expensive than for younger travelers, due to the increased risk of medical emergencies. However, there are a number of affordable plans available, and it’s important to compare quotes from multiple providers to find the best deal. When choosing travel insurance for seniors, it’s important to consider the following factors: coverage, cost, customer service, and the provider’s financial strength. It’s also important to read the fine print of the policy to understand what is and isn’t covered. If you have preexisting medical conditions, be sure to choose a plan that offers coverage for those conditions. It’s also a good idea to purchase travel insurance as soon as you book your trip, as this will ensure that you’re covered for any unforeseen events that may occur before your departure. What is the best travel insurance for seniors? The best travel insurance for seniors depends on your individual needs and budget. However, we recommend Allianz as our top pick for their optional preexisting condition coverage and excellent travel expense protection. Other good options include International Medical Group for international travelers, John Hancock for comprehensive coverage, Nationwide for cruise travel, and Travel Guard for specialized coverages. How much does travel insurance cost for seniors? The cost of travel insurance for seniors varies depending on a number of factors, including the traveler’s age, destination, length of trip, and the type of coverage selected. In general, travel insurance for seniors is more expensive than for younger travelers, due to the increased risk of medical emergencies. However, there are a number of affordable plans available, and it’s important to compare quotes from multiple providers to find the best deal. What does travel insurance cover? Travel insurance typically covers trip cancellation, trip interruption, trip delay, missed connection, baggage loss, baggage delay, emergency medical expenses, emergency medical evacuation, and accidental death and dismemberment. Some plans also offer coverage for preexisting medical conditions, rental car damage, and trip cancellation for any reason. It’s important to read the fine print of your policy to understand what is and isn’t covered. Do I need travel insurance if I have Medicare? Medicare typically does not cover health expenses incurred outside of the United States. Therefore, it’s important to purchase travel insurance if you’re traveling internationally, even if you have Medicare. Some travel insurance plans also offer coverage for trip cancellation, trip interruption, and other travel-related expenses, which Medicare does not cover. How do I file a travel insurance claim? To file a travel insurance claim, you will need to contact your insurance provider and provide them with the necessary documentation, such as receipts, medical records, and police reports. The claims process can vary depending on the provider, so it’s important to read your policy carefully and follow the instructions provided. Some providers allow you to file claims online, while others require you to submit claims by mail or fax.
